body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance` **Service directory:** `sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/` **Triggered by:** commit 1b854213504f49440f2dede6631cc3a35fe71571 (PR #57924) by @ArcturusZhang ## What Changed PR #57924 introduced the new `Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance` package (version `1.0.0-beta.1`), which exposes four main resource types for managing SAP workloads on Azure: `SapVirtualInstance`, `SapApplicationServerInstance`, `SapCentralServerInstance`, and `SapDatabaseInstance`. The package includes auto-generated samples in `tests/Generated/Samples/` but has no service-specific documentation. ## Gaps Found - **Key concepts section** is a single generic link — no description of the four core resource types (`SapVirtualInstance`, `SapApplicationServerInstance`, `SapCentralServerInstance`, `SapDatabaseInstance`) or what they represent - **Examples section** contains only a generic link to the Azure management samples catalog — no service-specific code snippets demonstrating CRUD operations on SAP Virtual Instances - **No `tests/Samples/` directory** — no snippet-backed sample test files exist; there are only auto-generated samples in `tests/Generated/Samples/` which cannot serve as README snippet sources - **README authentication section** is a redirect link only — no concrete `ArmClient` initialization example specific to this package
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/README.md` Replace the generic **Key concepts** section with: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### SapVirtualInstance `SapVirtualInstanceResource` represents the top-level SAP system deployed on Azure. It encompasses the full SAP landscape — including the ABAP Central Services, application servers, and database — and is the entry point for managing the complete SAP workload. ### SapApplicationServerInstance `SapApplicationServerInstanceResource` represents a single SAP Application Server (also called a dialog instance). An SAP Virtual Instance may contain one or more application server instances that handle end-user workloads. ### SapCentralServerInstance `SapCentralServerInstanceResource` represents the ABAP Central Services instance (ASCS) within an SAP Virtual Instance. It hosts the message server and enqueue server, providing high-availability coordination. ### SapDatabaseInstance `SapDatabaseInstanceResource` represents the database layer of an SAP Virtual Instance, such as an SAP HANA database instance. ### Thread safety We guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-service-methods-thread-safety)). This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads. ### Additional concepts [Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) | [Accessing the response](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#accessing-http-response-details-using-responset) | [Long-running operations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#consuming-long-running-operations-using-operationt) | [Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) | [Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/samples/Diagnostics.md) | [Mocking](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/sdk/unit-testing-mocking) | [Client lifetime](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/lifetime-management-and-thread-safety-guarantees-of-azure-sdk-net-clients/) ``` Replace the generic **Examples** section with: ```markdown ## Examples The following sections provide several code snippets covering some of the most common Azure Workloads SAP Virtual Instance tasks: - [Get a SAP Virtual Instance](#get-a-sap-virtual-instance) - [List all SAP Virtual Instances in a subscription](#list-all-sap-virtual-instances-in-a-subscription) - [List Application Server Instances](#list-application-server-instances) ### Get a SAP Virtual Instance ```C# Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_GetSapVirtualInstance ``` ### List all SAP Virtual Instances in a subscription ```C# Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListSapVirtualInstances ``` ### List Application Server Instances ```C# Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListApplicationServerInstances ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **Create** `sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/tests/Samples/Sample_SapVirtualInstance.cs` with the following content: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance.Samples { public class Sample_SapVirtualInstance { [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task GetSapVirtualInstanceAsync() { #region Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_GetSapVirtualInstance ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); // Replace with your subscription ID, resource group name, and SAP Virtual Instance name string subscriptionId = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"; string resourceGroupName = "myResourceGroup"; string sapVirtualInstanceName = "mySapVirtualInstance"; ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}")) .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName); SapVirtualInstanceResource sapVirtualInstance = await resourceGroup .GetSapVirtualInstances() .GetAsync(sapVirtualInstanceName); Console.WriteLine($"SAP Virtual Instance: {sapVirtualInstance.Data.Name}"); Console.WriteLine($"Environment: {sapVirtualInstance.Data.Environment}"); Console.WriteLine($"SAP Product: {sapVirtualInstance.Data.SapProduct}"); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task ListSapVirtualInstancesAsync() { #region Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListSapVirtualInstances ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); string subscriptionId = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"; SubscriptionResource subscription = await client .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}")) .GetAsync(); await foreach (SapVirtualInstanceResource svi in subscription.GetSapVirtualInstancesAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Name: {svi.Data.Name}, Location: {svi.Data.Location}"); } #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task ListApplicationServerInstancesAsync() { #region Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListApplicationServerInstances ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); string subscriptionId = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"; string resourceGroupName = "myResourceGroup"; string sapVirtualInstanceName = "mySapVirtualInstance"; ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}")) .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName); SapVirtualInstanceResource sapVirtualInstance = await resourceGroup .GetSapVirtualInstances() .GetAsync(sapVirtualInstanceName); await foreach (SapApplicationServerInstanceResource appServer in sapVirtualInstance.GetSapApplicationServerInstancesAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"App Server: {appServer.Data.Name}, Status: {appServer.Data.Status}"); } #endregion } } } ``` For each snippet: | Snippet name | Test file | README location | |---|---|---| | `WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_GetSapVirtualInstance` | `tests/Samples/Sample_SapVirtualInstance.cs` | Under `### Get a SAP Virtual Instance` | | `WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListSapVirtualInstances` | `tests/Samples/Sample_SapVirtualInstance.cs` | Under `### List all SAP Virtual Instances in a subscription` | | `WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListApplicationServerInstances` | `tests/Samples/Sample_SapVirtualInstance.cs` | Under `### List Application Server Instances` | ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/README.md` must contain these sections: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — present (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) 2. ❌ **Key concepts** — present but generic; needs service-specific resource type descriptions (see Step 1) 3. ❌ **Examples** — present but generic; needs service-specific snippets (see Steps 1 & 2) 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present (generic is acceptable for management SDKs) 5. ✅ **Next steps** — present 6. ✅ **Contributing** — present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 workloadssapvirtualinstance` — injects snippet code from `#region Snippet:` tags into README placeholders; check the git diff after running to confirm README was updated correctly 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 workloadssapvirtualinstance` — regenerates public API listing files; required if any public types or members changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/` — ensures consistent code formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance] Docs: Add service-specific README content and snippet-backed samples
